timeline.title: CIS Tender 7 underwriting awarded (283 MW)
timeline.detail: BayWa r.e.'s Bullewah (AEMO ID: Bullawah Wind Farm – Stage 1) wins CIS Tender 7 underwriting. Awarded capacity is 283 MW — constrained by the 283 MW SW REZ access right BayWa holds. Full ~804 MW proposed project (Stages 1 + 2) sits outside the T7 award.

sources.title: Australia's biggest wind project among 19 winners (RenewEconomy)

timeline.title: South-West REZ access right (283 MW)
timeline.detail: BayWa r.e. awarded 283 MW SW REZ access right from EnergyCo — less than the project's full proposed ~804 MW capacity.

scheme_contracts.contract_type: CISA (federal underwriting) — capped by REZ access right

stakeholder_issues.issue: 521 MW of proposed project sits outside T7 award
stakeholder_issues.detail: Full Bullewah / Bullawah project ~804 MW (Stages 1 + 2). CIS T7 covers 283 MW. The remaining ~521 MW could be a candidate for the Q2 2026 NSW state LTESA Generation round, but Stage 2 is wind-alone in a hybrid-favoured round.

stakeholder_issues.issue: Project name inconsistency — Bullewah vs Bullawah
stakeholder_issues.detail: RenewEconomy 2026-05-23 article refers to the project as 'Bullewah'; AEMO Generation Information uses 'Bullawah Wind Farm – Stage 1'. Same BayWa r.e. project, different spellings.
